regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment after surgery/biopsy when upfront RT and chemotherapy is not preferred, WHO grade 2, KPS ≥60; No residual/measurable disease after surgery/biopsy

trial_data.pfs: Median PFS 27.7 months vs. 11.1 months for placebo

regimens.notes: Vorasidenib is a dual inhibitor of IDH1 and IDH2. In the phase 3 INDIGO study (Mellinghoff IK, et al. N Engl J Med 2023;389:589-601), vorasidenib improved median PFS (27.7 months vs. 11.1 months) compared to placebo in patients with residual or recurrent grade 2 IDH1/2-mutant glioma after surgery and no prior treatment. Overall survival data from this study are not yet available. Newly diagnosed patients with oligodendroglioma/astrocytoma (WHO grade 2 or 3) who did not have residual disease were excluded from the INDIGO study; thus it remains unknown if this subset would benefit from immediate treatment with an IDH inhibitor. The Panel strongly recommends multidisciplinary discussion for patients with newly diagnosed gliomas or if treatment with an IDH inhibitor is being considered.

regimens.regimen_name: Ivosidenib monotherapy (for IDH1 mutation, alternative to vorasidenib)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment after surgery/biopsy when upfront RT and chemotherapy is not preferred, WHO grade 2, KPS ≥60; No residual/measurable disease after surgery/biopsy
regimens.notes: Ivosidenib is an IDH1 inhibitor and is a reasonable alternative if a patient cannot tolerate vorasidenib (Mellinghoff IK, et al. J Clin Oncol 2020;38:3398-3406). This option applies to IDH1-mutant tumors only.

regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment after surgery/biopsy, if initial treatment with RT and chemotherapy is preferred or after progression on IDH inhibitor, WHO grade 2, KPS ≥60

drugs.name: Procarbazine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol

drugs.name: Lomustine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol
drugs.schedule: Part of PCV regimen (carmustine may be substituted for lomustine)

drugs.name: Vincristine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol

trial_data.os: Significant improvement in median OS (median OS increased from 7.8 years to 13.3 years, P = .02); 10-year survival rate increased from 41% to 62%

regimens.notes: Based on RTOG 9802 results, RT followed by 6 cycles of PCV is a category 1 recommendation. When PCV is recommended, carmustine may be substituted for lomustine. The Panel recommends that PCV be administered after RT (as per EORTC 26951) since the intensive PCV regimen given prior to RT (RTOG 9402) was not tolerated as well. A retrospective subgroup analysis suggests that the survival benefit with the addition of PCV was seen only in IDH-mutant tumors; patients with oligodendrogliomas benefited more than those with astrocytomas.

regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment after surgery/biopsy, if initial treatment with RT and chemotherapy is preferred or after progression on IDH inhibitor, WHO grade 2, KPS ≥60
regimens.notes: RT followed by adjuvant temozolomide is an alternative to RT plus PCV. There is currently a lack of prospective randomized phase 3 data for the use of radiation and TMZ in patients with low-grade glioma, but data from EORTC and NCIC studies in glioblastoma support this regimen. PCV is generally more difficult to tolerate than TMZ, so it may be reasonable to treat elderly or comorbid patients with RT and TMZ instead.

regimens.regimen_name: RT with concurrent and adjuvant temozolomide
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment after surgery/biopsy, if initial treatment with RT and chemotherapy is preferred or after progression on IDH inhibitor, WHO grade 2, KPS ≥60

drugs.name: Temozolomide
drugs.schedule: Concurrent with RT and adjuvant following RT

regimens.notes: Data from EORTC and NCIC studies, which included patients with glioblastoma, support RT with concurrent and adjuvant TMZ as an evidence-based regimen. This is an alternative to RT plus PCV.

regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60

drugs.name: Procarbazine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol

drugs.name: Lomustine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol
drugs.schedule: Part of PCV regimen (carmustine may be substituted for lomustine)

drugs.name: Vincristine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol

trial_data.os: OS not reached in the RT + PCV group vs. 112 months in the RT group

regimens.notes: The addition of PCV to RT for the treatment of newly diagnosed WHO grade 3 oligodendrogliomas is supported by results from two phase III trials: EORTC 26951 and RTOG 9402. The Panel recommends that PCV be administered after RT (as per EORTC 26951) since the intensive PCV regimen given prior to RT (RTOG 9402) was not tolerated as well and resulted in two deaths from PCV-induced neutropenia. In EORTC 26951, 70% of patients in the RT followed by PCV arm did not complete the planned six cycles of treatment.

regimens.regimen_name: RT with neoadjuvant or adjuvant PCV
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60

drugs.name: Procarbazine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol
drugs.schedule: Part of PCV regimen (neoadjuvant or adjuvant)

drugs.name: Lomustine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol

drugs.name: Vincristine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol

regimens.notes: Both EORTC 26951 (adjuvant PCV after RT) and RTOG 9402 (neoadjuvant PCV before RT) demonstrated significant increases in median OS when PCV was added to RT. The Panel prefers adjuvant administration after RT based on better tolerability.

regimens.regimen_name: RT with concurrent and adjuvant temozolomide
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60

drugs.name: Temozolomide
drugs.schedule: Concurrent with RT and adjuvant following RT

regimens.notes: RT with concurrent and adjuvant temozolomide is an alternative option. The phase III CODEL study is ongoing to compare RT + PCV to RT + TMZ in patients with WHO grade 2 or 3 oligodendroglioma.

regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60
regimens.notes: RT and adjuvant temozolomide is an alternative option for WHO grade 3 oligodendroglioma.

regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment after surgery/biopsy, if initial treatment with RT and chemotherapy is preferred, WHO grade 2, KPS ≥60

drugs.name: Procarbazine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol

drugs.name: Lomustine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol

drugs.name: Vincristine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol

regimens.notes: RT followed by adjuvant PCV is a preferred option for IDH-mutant astrocytoma grade 2.

regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment after surgery/biopsy, if initial treatment with RT and chemotherapy is preferred, WHO grade 2, KPS ≥60
regimens.notes: RT followed by adjuvant temozolomide is an alternative option for IDH-mutant astrocytoma grade 2.

regimens.regimen_name: RT with concurrent and adjuvant temozolomide
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment after surgery/biopsy, if initial treatment with RT and chemotherapy is preferred, WHO grade 2, KPS ≥60

drugs.name: Temozolomide
drugs.schedule: Concurrent with RT and adjuvant following RT

regimens.notes: RT with concurrent and adjuvant temozolomide is an alternative option for IDH-mutant astrocytoma grade 2.

regimens.regimen_name: RT followed by adjuvant temozolomide (preferred) or RT with concurrent and adjuvant temozolomide
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60

drugs.name: Temozolomide
drugs.schedule: Adjuvant following RT, or concurrent with RT and adjuvant following RT

regimens.notes: RT with adjuvant temozolomide is preferred based on the CATNON trial results showing improvement in survival with RT followed by 12 cycles of TMZ compared to RT alone (van den Bent MJ, et al. Lancet Oncol 2021;22:813-823).

regimens.regimen_name: RT with concurrent and adjuvant temozolomide ± alternating electric field therapy
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment, WHO grade 4, KPS ≥60

drugs.name: Temozolomide
drugs.schedule: Concurrent with RT and adjuvant following RT

regimens.notes: Treatment of grade 4 disease is extrapolated from interim analyses of data from the CATNON study. Final results of CATNON are not yet available. Alternating electric field therapy is only an option for patients with supratentorial disease.

regimens.regimen_name: RT with concurrent and adjuvant temozolomide + alternating electric field therapy (TTF)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment, KPS ≥60, age ≤70 years

drugs.name: Temozolomide
drugs.dose: 75 mg/m2 daily concurrent with RT, then 150-200 mg/m2 post-irradiation on 5-day schedule every 28 days
drugs.schedule: Concurrent with RT and adjuvant following RT

biomarkers.requirement: Not required but prognostic/predictive; methylated or unmethylated

regimens.notes: In the EF-14 trial, patients with newly diagnosed glioblastoma and good PS (KPS ≥70) were randomized to TMZ alone or TMZ plus alternating electric field therapy following standard focal brain radiation and daily TMZ. The combination improved both median PFS and OS. Alternating electric field therapy is only an option for patients with supratentorial disease. Treatment with carmustine wafer may impact enrollment in adjuvant clinical trials.

regimens.regimen_name: RT with concurrent and adjuvant temozolomide
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment, KPS ≥60, age ≤70 years

drugs.name: Temozolomide
drugs.dose: 75 mg/m2 daily concurrent with RT, then 150-200 mg/m2 post-irradiation on 5-day schedule every 28 days
drugs.schedule: Concurrent with RT and adjuvant following RT

trial_data.os: Median OS 14.6 vs. 12.1 months; 5-year OS 10% vs. 2%

regimens.notes: Adjuvant involved-field RT with concurrent and adjuvant TMZ is the standard recommended treatment based on the EORTC-NCIC trial. The TMZ dose used is 75 mg/m2 daily concurrent with RT, then 150 to 200 mg/m2 on a 5-day schedule every 28 days. There are no clear data that treatment with temozolomide beyond 6 cycles is beneficial, even in patients with MGMT promoter-methylated disease.

regimens.regimen_name: Hypofractionated RT plus concurrent and adjuvant temozolomide
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment, KPS ≥60, age >70 years, MGMT promoter-methylated or indeterminate

drugs.name: Temozolomide
drugs.dose: 75 mg/m2 daily concurrent with RT, then 150-200 mg/m2 post-irradiation on 5-day schedule every 28 days
drugs.schedule: Concurrent with hypofractionated RT and adjuvant following RT

biomarkers.requirement: Preferred for methylated or indeterminate status

regimens.notes: For patients >70 years with MGMT promoter-methylated or indeterminate tumors, hypofractionated brain RT plus concurrent and adjuvant TMZ is a category 1 recommendation. The greatest improvement in median OS was seen in patients with MGMT promoter-methylated tumors (13.5 months RT + TMZ vs. 7.7 months RT alone; HR, 0.53; 95% CI, 0.38-0.73; P < .001).

regimens.regimen_name: Standard brain RT plus concurrent and adjuvant TMZ and alternating electric field therapy
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment, KPS ≥60, age >70 years, MGMT promoter-methylated or unmethylated/indeterminate

drugs.name: Temozolomide
drugs.dose: 75 mg/m2 daily concurrent with RT, then 150-200 mg/m2 post-irradiation on 5-day schedule every 28 days
drugs.schedule: Concurrent with RT and adjuvant following RT

biomarkers.requirement: Not required; category 1 for methylated, category 1 for unmethylated/indeterminate

regimens.notes: This is a reasonable option for patients >70 years of age who want to be treated as aggressively as possible.

regimens.regimen_name: Hypofractionated RT + concurrent or adjuvant temozolomide
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment, KPS <60, any age

drugs.name: Temozolomide
drugs.schedule: Concurrent or adjuvant to hypofractionated RT

regimens.notes: For patients aged ≤70 years with poor PS. Hypofractionated RT preferred.

regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment, KPS <60, MGMT promoter-methylated; or palliative setting

biomarkers.requirement: Preferred for patients with MGMT promoter-methylated tumors

regimens.notes: Temozolomide monotherapy is useful in certain circumstances for patients with MGMT promoter-methylated tumors and poor PS. Clinical benefit from temozolomide is likely to be lower in patients whose tumors lack MGMT promoter methylation.

regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment, KPS <60; or older adults (>70 years) with MGMT unmethylated tumors

biomarkers.requirement: May be used regardless of MGMT status; category 2B for unmethylated

regimens.notes: For patients with poor PS (KPS <60) or older adults with unmethylated MGMT tumors. Hypofractionated RT (eg, 34 Gy in 10 fractions or 40.05 Gy in 15 fractions).

regimens.setting: Poor PS (KPS <60)
regimens.notes: Palliative/best supportive care is an option for patients with newly diagnosed glioblastoma with poor PS.

regimens.site: Glioblastoma (IDH wild-type), Recurrent or Progressive Disease
regimens.setting: Recurrent or progressive disease, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60
regimens.notes: Bevacizumab is FDA approved for the treatment of recurrent glioblastoma based on improvement in PFS. Although no studies of bevacizumab in patients with recurrent glioblastoma have demonstrated an improvement in survival, bevacizumab may suppress vascular permeability and provide radiographic appearance of response. Patients who have evidence of radiographic progression may benefit from continuation of bevacizumab to prevent rapid neurologic deterioration.

regimens.site: Glioblastoma (IDH wild-type), Recurrent or Progressive Disease
regimens.setting: Recurrent or progressive disease, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60
regimens.notes: If there has been a long-time interval between stopping TMZ and development of tumor progression, it is reasonable to restart a patient on TMZ, particularly if the patient's tumor is MGMT methylated.

regimens.site: Glioblastoma (IDH wild-type), Recurrent or Progressive Disease
regimens.setting: Recurrent or progressive disease, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60
regimens.notes: Lomustine is a reasonable second-line therapy, especially in a patient whose tumor is MGMT methylated.

regimens.site: Glioblastoma (IDH wild-type), Recurrent or Progressive Disease
regimens.setting: Recurrent or progressive disease, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60
regimens.notes: Carmustine is a reasonable second-line therapy for recurrent glioblastoma.

regimens.site: Glioblastoma (IDH wild-type), Recurrent or Progressive Disease
regimens.setting: Recurrent or progressive disease, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60

drugs.name: Procarbazine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol

drugs.name: Lomustine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol
drugs.schedule: Part of PCV regimen (carmustine may be substituted for lomustine)

drugs.name: Vincristine
drugs.dose: PCV regimen doses as per protocol

regimens.notes: PCV is a preferred option for recurrent glioblastoma.

regimens.site: Glioblastoma (IDH wild-type), Recurrent or Progressive Disease
regimens.setting: Recurrent or progressive disease, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60
regimens.notes: Bevacizumab + systemic therapy can be considered if disease progression on bevacizumab monotherapy and it is desirable to continue the steroid-sparing effects of bevacizumab.

regimens.site: Glioblastoma (IDH wild-type), Recurrent or Progressive Disease
regimens.setting: Recurrent or progressive disease, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60
regimens.notes: Combination with bevacizumab may be considered for patients with recurrent disease.

regimens.site: Glioblastoma (IDH wild-type), Recurrent or Progressive Disease
regimens.setting: Recurrent or progressive disease, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60
regimens.notes: Combination with bevacizumab may be considered for patients with recurrent disease.

regimens.site: Glioblastoma (IDH wild-type), Recurrent or Progressive Disease
regimens.setting: Recurrent or progressive disease, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60; if disease progression or intolerance to preferred or other recommended regimens
regimens.notes: Etoposide is useful in certain circumstances for recurrent glioblastoma.

regimens.site: Glioblastoma (IDH wild-type), Recurrent or Progressive Disease
regimens.setting: Recurrent or progressive disease, WHO grade 3, KPS ≥60; if disease progression or intolerance to preferred or other recommended regimens
regimens.notes: Carboplatin (category 3) is useful in certain circumstances for recurrent glioblastoma.

regimens.setting: Brain metastases from NTRK1/2/3 gene fusion-positive solid tumors
regimens.notes: Larotrectinib is an NTRK inhibitor active in patients with NTRK gene fusion-positive solid tumors with brain metastases (Hong DS, et al. Lancet Oncol 2020;21:531-540).

regimens.setting: Brain metastases from NTRK1/2/3 gene fusion-positive solid tumors
regimens.notes: Entrectinib is an NTRK inhibitor active in patients with NTRK gene fusion-positive solid tumors with brain metastases (Doebele RC, et al. Lancet Oncol 2020;21:271-282).

regimens.setting: Brain metastases from NTRK1/2/3 gene fusion-positive solid tumors
regimens.notes: Repotrectinib is an NTRK inhibitor for NTRK gene fusion-positive solid tumors.

regimens.setting: Brain metastases from BRAF non-specific melanoma

biomarkers.requirement: Not required for selection; preferred for BRAF non-specific melanoma

regimens.notes: Two phase II trials support the use of combination ipilimumab and nivolumab for patients with asymptomatic untreated brain metastases from melanoma. In CheckMate 204, the intracranial response was 57.4% with complete response of 33%. The median duration of intracranial response was not reached at time of publication. Grade 3 or 4 treatment-related adverse events occurred in just over half of the patients.

regimens.setting: Brain metastases from BRAF V600E-positive melanoma
regimens.notes: The nonrandomized phase II COMBI-MB trial demonstrated clinical benefit and acceptable toxicity for dabrafenib/trametinib in patients with BRAF V600-mutant melanoma brain metastases. Among patients with asymptomatic brain metastases, an intracranial response was observed in 58% of those with untreated metastases.

regimens.setting: Brain metastases from BRAF V600E-positive melanoma
regimens.notes: Although there are no published prospective studies on the combination of vemurafenib and cobimetinib for melanoma patients with brain metastases, there is high-quality evidence that for melanoma with distant metastasis, combination therapy with vemurafenib and cobimetinib is associated with improved outcomes and safety compared with vemurafenib monotherapy.

regimens.setting: Brain metastases from EGFR-sensitizing mutation-positive NSCLC
regimens.notes: In the randomized phase III FLAURA trial, osimertinib was compared to a different EGFR-TKI (gefitinib or erlotinib) in 556 patients with previously untreated EGFR-sensitizing mutation-positive NSCLC. Patients with CNS metastases had median PFS of 15.2 months vs. 9.6 months (HR, 0.47; P < .001). CNS ORR was 91% vs. 68% (P = .066).

regimens.setting: Brain metastases from ALK gene fusion-positive NSCLC
regimens.notes: In a randomized phase III trial comparing brigatinib to crizotinib, intracranial response was more likely in the brigatinib arm than in the crizotinib arm (67% vs. 17%). Twelve-month survival without intracranial disease progression was 67% for brigatinib vs. 21% for crizotinib (HR, 0.27; 95% CI, 0.13-0.54).

regimens.setting: Brain metastases from ALK gene fusion-positive NSCLC
regimens.notes: Post hoc analysis of the CROWN trial showed that in patients with brain metastases at baseline, complete CNS response was observed in 61% of patients who received lorlatinib, compared to 15% in those who received crizotinib.

regimens.setting: Brain metastases from ALK gene fusion-positive NSCLC
regimens.notes: In a randomized phase III trial comparing alectinib to crizotinib, CNS response was observed in 81% of patients in the alectinib arm (8 complete responses) and 50% in the crizotinib arm (1 complete response).

regimens.setting: Brain metastases from HER2 (ERBB2) positive breast cancer, previously treated with ≥1 regimen
regimens.notes: In the HER2CLIMB phase III trial, tucatinib combined with trastuzumab and capecitabine was compared to placebo plus trastuzumab and capecitabine in patients with HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer previously treated with HER2-directed therapy. Among patients with brain metastases at baseline, both PFS and OS were superior in the tucatinib arm.

regimens.setting: Brain metastases from HER2 (ERBB2) positive breast cancer, previously treated with ≥1 regimen
regimens.notes: Subgroup analysis from the DESTINY-Breast01 trial showed activity of fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki in patients with HER2-positive breast cancer with brain metastases.

regimens.site: Small Cell Lung Cancer with Brain Metastases
regimens.setting: Brain metastases from SCLC
regimens.notes: Tarlatamab-dlle is a bispecific T-cell engager for the treatment of SCLC.

regimens.site: Renal Cell Carcinoma with Brain Metastases
regimens.setting: Brain metastases from renal cell carcinoma
regimens.notes: Cabozantinib has demonstrated activity in patients with RCC and brain metastases.

regimens.setting: Induction therapy, newly diagnosed
regimens.notes: High doses of intravenous methotrexate (≥3.5 g/m2) are necessary to overcome the BBB and achieve therapeutic levels in the CSF. Consider glucarpidase for prolonged methotrexate clearance due to methotrexate-induced renal toxicity.

regimens.regimen_name: High-Dose Methotrexate 8 g/m2 combined with Rituximab
regimens.setting: Induction therapy, newly diagnosed
regimens.notes: High-dose methotrexate combined with rituximab is a preferred induction regimen. Hepatitis B testing is indicated because of the risk of reactivation with immunotherapy plus systemic therapy.

regimens.setting: Induction therapy, newly diagnosed; consider WBRT
regimens.notes: MPV + Rituximab is a preferred induction regimen for PCNSL.

regimens.setting: Induction therapy, newly diagnosed
regimens.notes: MT + Rituximab is a preferred induction regimen for PCNSL.

regimens.setting: Induction therapy, newly diagnosed
regimens.notes: MATRIX is associated with significant myeloid toxicity. This regimen is based on the IELSG32 trial results (Ferreri AJM, et al. Lancet Haematol 2016;3:e217-227).

regimens.regimen_name: Cytarabine/Thiotepa followed by Carmustine/Thiotepa (high-dose systemic therapy with stem cell rescue)
regimens.setting: Consolidation therapy after CR or CRu
regimens.notes: High-dose systemic therapy with stem cell rescue is a preferred consolidation option after CR or CRu. The risk of neurotoxicity should be considered before administering high-dose therapy to a patient with prior WBRT.

regimens.regimen_name: TBC (Thiotepa/Busulfan/Cyclophosphamide/Mesna) (high-dose systemic therapy with stem cell rescue)
regimens.setting: Consolidation therapy after CR or CRu
regimens.notes: TBC is a preferred high-dose consolidation regimen with stem cell rescue.

regimens.setting: Consolidation therapy after CR or CRu
regimens.notes: EA consolidation was evaluated in the Alliance 50202 trial.

regimens.setting: Consolidation therapy after CR or CRu
regimens.notes: High-dose cytarabine is a preferred consolidation option.

regimens.setting: Relapsed or refractory disease
regimens.notes: Ibrutinib is associated with risk of Aspergillus infection. This is a recommended regimen for relapsed/refractory PCNSL.

regimens.setting: Relapsed or refractory disease
regimens.notes: MBVP + Rituximab is a recommended regimen for relapsed/refractory PCNSL.

regimens.setting: Relapsed or refractory disease
regimens.notes: Lenalidomide + Rituximab is a recommended regimen for relapsed/refractory PCNSL.

regimens.setting: Relapsed or refractory disease
regimens.notes: Tisagenlecleucel was added to the guidelines for relapsed/refractory PCNSL based on a phase 1/2 clinical trial (Frigault MJ, et al. Blood 2022;139:2306-2315).

regimens.setting: Relapsed or refractory disease
regimens.notes: Axicabtagene ciloleucel was added based on a pilot study in relapsed/refractory primary and secondary CNS lymphoma (Nayak L, et al. ASCO Annual Meeting 2024).

regimens.setting: Relapsed or refractory disease
regimens.notes: Ibrutinib/Lenalidomide + Rituximab was added based on a phase Ib study with expansion (Schaff LR, et al. Neuro Oncol 2025;27:2107-2116).

regimens.setting: Recurrent/progressive meningioma
regimens.notes: A prospective, multicenter, nonrandomized, phase II trial evaluating sunitinib in 36 heavily pretreated patients with refractory meningioma showed a 6-month PFS rate of 42%, with a median PFS rate of 5.2 months and a median OS rate of 24.6 months (Kaley TJ, et al. Neuro Oncol 2015;17:116-121). Toxicities were considerable, with 60% of patients experiencing grade 3 or higher toxicity.

regimens.setting: Recurrent/progressive meningioma
regimens.notes: Abemaciclib was added to the guidelines based on a multicenter basket trial for CNS tumors that identified activity of the CDK4/6 inhibitor abemaciclib in recurrent meningioma (Kaley TJ, et al. Neuro Oncol 2025;27:3189-3199).

regimens.setting: Recurrent/progressive meningioma
regimens.notes: Retrospective data support the use of bevacizumab for patients with recurrent meningioma, especially for patients with symptoms driven by RT necrosis, with a 6-month PFS rate of 43.8% for recurrent surgery and radiation-refractory grade 2 and 3 meningioma.

regimens.setting: Recurrent/progressive meningioma
regimens.notes: Octreotide acetate is a somatostatin analogue useful in certain circumstances for recurrent meningioma.

regimens.setting: Recurrent/progressive meningioma
regimens.notes: In a phase II trial evaluating bevacizumab combined with everolimus for recurrent meningioma (N = 17), stable disease was reported in 88% of patients, with median PFS and OS of 22.0 months and 23.8 months, respectively.

regimens.setting: Leptomeningeal metastases from EGFR mutation-positive NSCLC (for exon 19 deletion or L858R)
regimens.notes: Amivantamab and hyaluronidase-lpuj subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV amivantamab-vmjw. This combination has different dosing and administration instructions compared to IV amivantamab-vmjw.

regimens.setting: Leptomeningeal metastases from HER2-positive breast cancer
regimens.notes: Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki is the preferred systemic therapy option for leptomeningeal metastases from HER2-positive breast cancer.

regimens.setting: Leptomeningeal metastases from melanoma
regimens.notes: Intrathecal and intravenous nivolumab was evaluated in a phase 1 trial for melanoma with leptomeningeal disease (Glitza Oliva IC, et al. Nat Med 2023;29:898-905).

regimens.setting: Progressive or symptomatic vestibular schwannoma
regimens.notes: Treatment with bevacizumab results in hearing improvement in 36% of patients with NF2-SWN with hearing loss due to VS, stabilization in 46%, and deterioration in 18%. Treatment results in a partial VS volume reduction (≥20%) on MRI in 40% of patients and disease stabilization in 50%, while 10% experienced tumor progression.

regimens.setting: Progressive or symptomatic vestibular schwannoma
regimens.notes: Brigatinib was evaluated in a study of NF2-related schwannomatosis with progressive tumors (Plotkin SR, et al. N Engl J Med 2024;390:2284-2294).

regimens.setting: VHL-associated CNS hemangioblastomas not requiring immediate surgery
regimens.notes: Belzutifan has been FDA-approved for the treatment of VHL-associated CNS hemangioblastomas not requiring immediate surgery. Based on a nonrandomized phase 2 trial including patients with VHL-associated renal cell carcinoma (N = 61), objective response in patients with CNS hemangioblastoma was 30% (Jonasch E, et al. N Engl J Med 2021;385:2036-2046).

regimens.site: Primary Spinal Cord Tumors / NF1-associated plexiform neurofibromas
regimens.setting: Symptomatic plexiform neurofibromas not amenable to complete resection
regimens.notes: Selumetinib is a category 1 recommendation for patients with NF1 who have symptomatic plexiform neurofibromas not amenable to complete resection.

regimens.site: Primary Spinal Cord Tumors / NF1-associated plexiform neurofibromas
regimens.setting: Symptomatic plexiform neurofibromas not amenable to complete resection
regimens.notes: Mirdametinib was evaluated in a pivotal phase IIb trial (ReNeu) in adults and children with symptomatic NF1-associated plexiform neurofibromas (Moertel CL, et al. J Clin Oncol 2025;43:716-729).

regimens.regimen_name: Dabrafenib/Trametinib for circumscribed glioma with BRAF alterations
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment for PA, circumscribed ganglioglioma/glioneuronal tumor, PXA (grade 2) with BRAF V600E activation mutation; or recurrent disease with BRAF alterations
regimens.notes: The phase II VE-BASKET study showed that vemurafenib was efficacious in BRAF-mutated low-grade gliomas, particularly PXA. A phase II trial including 13 patients with BRAF-mutated low-grade glioma showed that dabrafenib/trametinib was associated with an ORR of 69%.

regimens.regimen_name: Cobimetinib/Vemurafenib for circumscribed glioma with BRAF alterations
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment for PA, circumscribed ganglioglioma/glioneuronal tumor, PXA (grade 2) with BRAF V600E activation mutation; or recurrent disease with BRAF alterations
regimens.notes: Cobimetinib/Vemurafenib is useful in certain circumstances for BRAF V600E-mutant circumscribed gliomas.

regimens.regimen_name: Larotrectinib for circumscribed glioma with NTRK1/2/3 gene fusions
regimens.setting: Recurrent or progressive disease with NTRK1/2/3 gene fusions
regimens.notes: Larotrectinib is an NTRK inhibitor for tumors with NTRK gene fusions.

regimens.regimen_name: Entrectinib for circumscribed glioma with NTRK1/2/3 gene fusions
regimens.setting: Recurrent or progressive disease with NTRK1/2/3 gene fusions
regimens.notes: Entrectinib is an NTRK inhibitor for tumors with NTRK gene fusions.

regimens.regimen_name: Repotrectinib for circumscribed glioma with NTRK1/2/3 gene fusions
regimens.setting: Recurrent or progressive disease with NTRK1/2/3 gene fusions
regimens.notes: Repotrectinib is an NTRK inhibitor for tumors with NTRK gene fusions.

regimens.regimen_name: Tovorafenib for BRAF gene fusion or BRAF V600 mutation
regimens.setting: Recurrent or progressive disease with BRAF gene fusion or BRAF V600 mutation

biomarkers.marker: BRAF gene fusion or BRAF V600 mutation

regimens.notes: Tovorafenib is a type II RAF inhibitor evaluated in relapsed/refractory pediatric low-grade glioma in the phase 2 FIREFLY-1 trial (Kilburn LB, et al. Nat Med 2024;30:207-217).

regimens.setting: Recurrent disease
regimens.notes: Temozolomide was evaluated in a retrospective study for salvage treatment of recurrent intracranial ependymomas in adults (Ruda R, et al. Neuro Oncol 2016;18:261-268).

regimens.setting: Recurrent disease
regimens.notes: Platinum-based regimens including carboplatin/etoposide have been used for recurrent ependymoma.

regimens.setting: Recurrent disease
regimens.notes: Bevacizumab was evaluated for recurrent ependymoma (Green RM, et al. Neurology 2009;73:1677-1680).

regimens.setting: Recurrent disease
regimens.notes: The combination of lapatinib and dose-dense temozolomide was evaluated in a phase II trial in patients with recurrent grade 1, 2, and 3 ependymoma (Gilbert MR, et al. Neuro Oncol 2021;23:468-477).

regimens.regimen_name: Cisplatin/Cyclophosphamide/Vincristine (following weekly vincristine with concurrent radiation during craniospinal RT)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment following craniospinal RT for standard-risk and high-risk disease
regimens.notes: Omission of vincristine during the radiotherapy phase of therapy or dose modification may be required for adults because they do not tolerate this regimen as well. Data supporting vincristine's use have been found in pediatric trials only.

regimens.regimen_name: Cisplatin/Lomustine/Vincristine (following weekly vincristine with concurrent radiation during craniospinal RT)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant treatment following craniospinal RT for standard-risk and high-risk disease
regimens.notes: Omission of vincristine during the radiotherapy phase of therapy or dose modification may be required for adults because they do not tolerate this regimen as well.

regimens.setting: Recurrent disease, no prior systemic therapy or prior systemic therapy
regimens.notes: High-dose Cyclophosphamide/Etoposide is recommended for recurrent medulloblastoma in patients with no prior or prior systemic therapy.

regimens.regimen_name: Vismodegib (for SHH pathway-mutant medulloblastoma with prior systemic therapy)
regimens.setting: Recurrent disease with SHH pathway mutation and prior systemic therapy
regimens.notes: Patients in trials with SHH-activated disease were more likely to respond than patients with non-SHH disease (Robinson GW, et al. J Clin Oncol 2015;33:2646-2654).

regimens.setting: Recurrent H3K27M-mutant diffuse glioma
regimens.notes: Dordaviprone is a caseinolytic protease P (ClpP) agonist and dopamine receptor D2 (DRD2) antagonist. In adult and pediatric patients with recurrent H3 K27M-mutant diffuse glioma who were evaluated by Blinded Independent Central Review (BICR)-confirmed RANO 2.0 criteria, dordaviprone monotherapy demonstrated a 28.0% overall response rate (ORR), 4.6-month median time to recovery (mTTR), 10.4-month median duration of response (mDOR), and 13.7-month median overall survival (Arrillaga-Romany I, et al. J Clin Oncol 2024;42:1542-1552).
